Navigation.t0: "Where the roots of faith and the wings of knowledge grow together."

Hero.hadithQuote: "Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.." [Hadith]
Hero.description: Rawdatul Atfaal provides structured learning in Qur'an, Hadith, Aqeedah, Fiqh, and essential academic subjects, helping children grow with knowledge, discipline, and Islamic character.

story.quote: "We don't just teach children. We nurture the people they are becoming."
story.p1: Founded on a simple but profound belief: a Muslim child deserves both a strong Islamic identity AND a rigorous academic education. There should be no compromise between deen and duniya.
story.p2: At Rawdatul Atfaal, we demand excellence in both, raising a generation grounded in their faith and fully equipped to navigate and lead in the modern world.

islamic.title: THE ISLAMIC PROGRAMME
islamic.courses: Qur'an | Arabic | Tawheed | Fiqh | Hadith | Mutoon

t1.quote: My son's tajweed has improved so much, and yet he still loves building robots in the programming class. This school is exactly what we were looking for.
t1.author: ZAYNAB A.

t2.quote: I used to worry about the balance between Islam and academics. Now I see my daughter thriving in both. It's a weight off our shoulders.
t2.author: OMAR F.

t3.quote: The teachers truly care. It's not just a school; it feels like a community that supports our family's values.
t3.author: FATIMA S.

fees.title: Transparent, Fair Pricing
fees.description: No hidden fees. No surprises. Just exceptional education. Families first.

foundation they deserve."

hero.title: Our Story
hero.quote: "Nurturing a generation grounded in their faith, fully equipped for the world."

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.p1: To provide an uncompromising educational experience where traditional Islamic sciences and modern academics are taught with equal rigor and excellence.
mission.p2: We strive to nurture students who are proud of their Muslim identity, deeply connected to the Qur'an, and capable of leading with integrity in any field they choose to pursue.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.p1: To be the standard-bearer for holistic Islamic education, producing graduates who embody the prophetic character (Akhlaq).
vision.p2: We envision a future where our alumni stand as pillars in their communities — possessing deep knowledge of their Deen and demonstrating exceptional competence in worldly sciences.

philosophy.subtitle: Our Philosophy
philosophy.title: No Compromise Between Deen & Duniya
philosophy.p1: For too long, Muslim families have been forced into a false dichotomy: choose a rigorous academic school with a secular environment, or choose a traditional Islamic school that lacks academic competitiveness.
philosophy.p3: Our dual-curriculum approach ensures that when a child is learning mathematics or programming, they are doing so with the same intensity as when they are memorizing the Qur'an or studying Fiqh. Excellence is not segmented; it is a holistic mindset we cultivate across all disciplines.

values.title: Our Core Values
values.v1_desc: Striving for perfection in both our worship and our studies, knowing Allah loves when a task is done well.
values.v2_desc: Upholding honesty, immense responsibility, and moral uprightness in every action.
values.v3_desc: Unapologetically rooting our knowledge and methodology in the Qur'an and the authentic Sunnah.
values.v4_desc: Fostering deeply rooted care, empathy, and community among students, staff, and parents.

origin.title: How We Began
origin.p1: Rawdatul Atfaal was founded circa 2021 as the foundational children's school under the umbrella of Markazul Bayaan. What started as a focused initiative has since blossomed into a global learning community, answering the call of families across the US, UK, Australia, Germany, Canada, India, Nigeria, Ghana, and beyond.
origin.p2: Our Islamic curriculum is firmly rooted in authenticity. We are proud to feature highly qualified Islamic tutors who have spent years studying under the recognized scholars of this era, adhering strictly to the methodology of the pious predecessors (Salaf).
origin.p3: Simultaneously, we ensure no compromise in secular academics. Our Western education department is spearheaded by highly experienced educators specializing in mathematics, programming, and the sciences, giving our students the absolute best of both worlds.
origin.quote: "We didn't just want to build a school; we wanted to revive a tradition of holistic, unapologetic scholarship."

hero.title: The Islamic Programme
hero.desc: Rooted in the authentic understanding of the pious predecessors, our curriculum guides students from foundational knowledge to advanced classical sciences.

quran.name: Qurʾān
quran.desc: Memorization (Hifdh), Tajweed, and Tafseer to connect deeply with the literal word of Allah.

arabic.name: Arabic Language
arabic.desc: Grammar (Nahw), Morphology (Sarf), and Literature (Adab) to understand the Deen from its original sources.

tawheed.name: Tawheed
tawheed.desc: Understanding the oneness of Allah and the core beliefs of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah.

fiqh.name: Fiqh
fiqh.desc: Islamic Jurisprudence focusing on worship (Ibadat) and daily transactions (Mu'amalat).

hadith.name: Hadith
hadith.desc: Studying the prophetic traditions, their sciences, and practical application.

mutoon.name: Mutoon
mutoon.desc: Memorization and explanation of classical foundational texts across various Islamic sciences.

hero.title: Arabic Language Curriculum
hero.quote: "The language of the Qurʼān — taught with care, structure, and love"

overview.title: Programme Overview
overview.p1: The Rawdatul Atfaal Arabic Language Curriculum is a structured five-level programme designed to take a student from their very first encounter with the Arabic alphabet all the way through to a confident reading of grammatical Arabic texts.
overview.p2: Arabic is the language of the Qurʼān, the hadith, and classical Islamic scholarship. A child who learns Arabic properly is a child who can engage with the primary sources of their religion directly, without the barrier of translation.
overview.p3: Our programme treats Arabic not merely as a foreign language to be learnt — but as a sacred inheritance to be reclaimed.

ProgArabic.t0: Upon completing Level 5, students will have a solid grounding in Arabic grammar and will be ready to progress to Madīnah Arabic Book 3 and beyond — opening the door to reading classical Islamic texts, Qurʼānic tafsīr, and hadith literature directly in Arabic.
ProgArabic.t1: To build upon the grammar foundation of Book 1 and introduce intermediate Arabic grammar structures: present tense verbs, relative pronouns, active/passive participles, dual forms, imperatives, and independent composition.
ProgArabic.t2: Arabic is the language of the Qurʼān, the hadith, and classical Islamic scholarship. A child who learns Arabic properly is a child who can engage with the primary sources of their religion directly, without the barrier of translation.
ProgArabic.t3: Our programme treats Arabic not merely as a foreign language to be learnt — but as a sacred inheritance to be reclaimed.
ProgArabic.t4: Give your child the key to the Qurʼān. Enrol them in the comprehensive 5-level Arabic Language curriculum today.
ProgArabic.t5: Command intermediate Arabic grammar — present tense, imperatives, plurals, relative clauses
ProgArabic.t6: Hold simple Arabic conversations, name objects and people, use basic sentence structures
ProgArabic.t7: Read and understand basic Arabic grammar — pronouns, nouns, past tense verbs, adjectives
ProgArabic.t8: Recognise and correctly pronounce all 28 Arabic letters with full diacritical marks
ProgArabic.t9: "The language of the Qurʼān — taught with care, structure, and love"
ProgArabic.t10: Join letters into words and read short Qurʼānic phrases with confidence

hero.title: Qurʼān Memorisation Programme
hero.subtitle: Juzʼ ʿAmma  |  Terms 1–4  |  Basic Programme
hero.quote: "Connecting young hearts to the Book of Allah"

overview.title: Programme Overview
overview.p1: The Rawdatul Atfaal Qurʼān Memorisation Programme guides students through the systematic memorisation of Juzʼ ʿAmma — the thirtieth part of the Qurʼān — across four structured terms.
overview.p2: Each term covers a defined range of surahs, progressing from the shorter surahs at the end of the Qurʼān toward the longer surahs earlier in Juzʼ ʿAmma.
overview.p3: Every term concludes with a dedicated revision week followed by a formal examination, ensuring that memorised material is retained and built upon.

ProgQuran.t0: Each term covers a defined range of surahs, progressing from the shorter surahs at the end of the Qurʼān toward the longer surahs earlier in Juzʼ ʿAmma.
ProgQuran.t1: Every term concludes with a dedicated revision week followed by a formal examination, ensuring that memorised material is retained and built upon.
ProgQuran.t2: Enrol your child in our complete Qurʼān memorisation programme. The journey of a lifetime starts with a single verse.

ProgTawheed.t8: "Equipping young Muslims with sound knowledge of their religion"
ProgTawheed.t10: The Rawdatul Atfaal Tawheed Curriculum is a structured, progressive programme designed to instill in young learners a firm and correct understanding of Islamic monotheism (Tawheed).
ProgTawheed.t11: Rooted in the renowned Saudi Arabia school curriculum, the programme guides students through five carefully sequenced levels — from foundational beliefs about Allah's Lordship to a comprehensive study of Al-Usool Ath-Thalaathah.
ProgTawheed.t12: Each level builds upon the previous, ensuring depth of understanding, retention of core Islamic principles, and an unshakeable belief system.
ProgTawheed.t26: Fundamentals of the deen & pillars of Islam
ProgTawheed.t30: Levels of the religion in depth
ProgTawheed.t49: To study, memorize and understand the foundational text Al-Usool Ath-Thalaathah in its entirety.
ProgTawheed.t51: This level marks the pinnacle of the Tawheed curriculum. Moving beyond topical units, the entire 16-week term is dedicated to the comprehensive study of the fundamental creedal manual,
ProgTawheed.t53: (The Three Fundamental Principles), authored by Shaykh al-Islam Muhammad ibn 'Abdil-Wahhaab.
ProgTawheed.t54: Across all sessions, students will engage in:
ProgTawheed.t55: Committing the Arabic text to memory with correct pronunciation.
ProgTawheed.t56: Understanding the direct evidences from the Qur'an and Sunnah.
ProgTawheed.t57: Grasping the reality of who Allah is, the religion of Islam, and the Prophet Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وسلم).
ProgTawheed.t58: Mastery of this foundational text ensures the student possesses an unshakable creed, equipped to answer the three questions of the grave.
ProgTawheed.t61: of Shaykh al-Islam Muhammad ibn 'Abdil-Wahhaab — encompassing the complete study of the text across all class sessions.
ProgTawheed.t63: Enrol your child in our complete Tawheed curriculum to secure their foundation in monotheism, protecting their faith from an early age.

ProgFiqh.t4: "Nurturing sound worship through authentic Fiqh education"
ProgFiqh.t6: The Rawdatul Atfaal Fiqh Curriculum is a progressive programme in Islamic jurisprudence (Fiqh), focused deeply on the chapter of worship (ʿIbaadaat).
ProgFiqh.t7: Beginning with the fundamentals of purification and prayer, the curriculum advances systematically through the rulings of ablution, conditions and pillars of Salah, congregational prayer, voluntary prayers and beyond.
ProgFiqh.t8: Each level effectively revises prior knowledge before introducing new material, ensuring students develop a sound and lasting understanding of how to worship Allah correctly.
ProgFiqh.t16: Purification, Ablution, Obligatory Prayers, Tashahhud, Nullifiers of Prayer
ProgFiqh.t19: Cleanliness, Ablution & nullifiers, Opening supplication, Prayer
ProgFiqh.t22: Lavatory manners, Removal of impurity, Conditions of wudhooʼ, Tayammum, Prayer pillars
ProgFiqh.t25: Full wudhooʼ, Adhaan, Prayer conditions, Surah Al-Faatihah, Tashahhud explanation
ProgFiqh.t28: Water classification, Khuff wiping, Congregational prayer, Voluntary prayers
ProgFiqh.t31: Enrol your child in our complete Fiqh curriculum to master the authentic rulings of Islamic worship, directly structured from the classical foundational texts.

ProgHadith.t4: "Building character and knowledge through the Sunnah of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم"
ProgHadith.t6: The Rawdatul Atfaal Hadith Curriculum is a four-level programme designed to connect young Muslims with the teachings of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وسلم through authentic hadith.
ProgHadith.t7: The programme progresses from foundational values and noble character in Level 1, through spiritual discipline and social responsibility in Levels 2 and 3.
ProgHadith.t8: It culminates in the complete memorisation and study of the renowned
ProgHadith.t10: in Level 4. At every stage, students are equipped to understand, internalise, and live by the prophetic guidance.
ProgHadith.t18: Heart purification, Sunnah, Prayer, Qurʼaan, Character, Brotherhood, Etiquette
ProgHadith.t21: Prayer refinement, Mosque etiquette, Tongue, Repentance, Charity, Dhikr, Knowledge
ProgHadith.t24: Tawheed, Bidʼah, Consistency, Patience, Ghurbah, Judgement Day, Lasting deeds
ProgHadith.t27: Complete memorisation & explanation of Imam An-Nawawee's Forty Hadith
ProgHadith.t30: Enrol your child in our complete Hadith curriculum to foster deep-rooted character and Islamic identity modeled directly from the Prophetic Sunnah.
ProgHadith.t45: The Forty Hadith of Imam An-Nawawī
ProgHadith.t54: Complete study, understanding, and memorisation of the Forty Hadith collection of Imam An-Nawawee — one of the most important collections in Islamic scholarship, covering the foundations of the religion.
ProgHadith.t56: This level differs from Levels 1–3 in structure. Rather than thematic topic units, the entire 16-week term is dedicated to the complete study of
ProgHadith.t58: — the celebrated collection of forty foundational hadith compiled by Imam Yahyaa ibn Sharaf An-Nawawee (631–676 AH, may Allah have mercy on him).
ProgHadith.t59: Each session is devoted to one or more hadith, covering:
ProgHadith.t60: Memorisation of the Arabic text of the hadith
ProgHadith.t61: Translation and clear explanation of the meaning
ProgHadith.t62: Lessons and principles derived from the hadith
ProgHadith.t63: Application to daily life and worship
ProgHadith.t64: This collection covers the pillars of Islam and Emaan, sincerity of intention, halal and haram, rights of others, the hereafter, and the etiquettes of seeking knowledge, making it the ideal capstone text for the Rawdatul Atfaal Hadith programme.
ProgHadith.t66: Advanced Islamic Principles & Maturity in Practice

ProgMutoon.t8: "Rooting students in the classical texts of Islamic scholarship"
ProgMutoon.t10: The Rawdatul Atfaal Mutoon Curriculum dedicates a portion of weekly study explicitly to the memorisation, understanding, and application of classical Islamic texts (mutoon).
ProgMutoon.t11: These are the identical foundational texts that have been studied in structured Islamic institutions for centuries. Beginning with short but highly consequential works on Tawheed and its nullifiers, students progress through increasingly substantive texts.
ProgMutoon.t12: By building this repository of memorised knowledge, we aim to furnish our children with protective theological frameworks that will serve them throughout their lives.
ProgMutoon.t14: Each week follows a strictly consistent pedagogical pattern. This rhythm ensures that new material is effectively consolidated before the student advances, preventing knowledge from accumulating without deep understanding.
ProgMutoon.t16: Focusing strictly on new sections, accurate recitation, and detailed comprehension of the classical texts.
ProgMutoon.t18: A completely dedicated day explicitly for reviewing the previous 3 days, solidifying retention, testing memory, and active Q&A.
ProgMutoon.t27: Memorise & understand the 10 nullifiers of Islam with their evidences
ProgMutoon.t31: Study the 4 principles distinguishing Tawheed from shirk, ancient and modern
ProgMutoon.t33: Progressing into Al-Usool Ath-Thalaathah & Kitaab At-Tawheed (Development phase)
ProgMutoon.t37: The Rawdatul Atfaal Mutoon programme follows a deliberate upward trajectory. While Levels 1 & 2 establish the immediate protective boundaries, subsequent levels will immerse students in deeper foundational treatises, subject to teacher discretion and student readiness.
ProgMutoon.t45: "And say: My Lord, increase me in knowledge."
ProgMutoon.t47: Enrol your child in our Mutoon foundational curriculum. Secure their faith with immutable texts memorised generation after generation.
ProgMutoon.t62: Week 5 — The Third Principle (continued) & The Fourth Principle
ProgMutoon.t63: Week 6 — Fourth Principle Completion & Full Review
ProgMutoon.t65: Week 3 — Examination of the Two Doubts

ProgMathematics.t9: "Spiral Learning • Conceptual Mastery • Algebraic Thinking • University Readiness"
ProgMathematics.t11: The Mathematics curriculum begins with the Horizons Math Curriculum (HMC) in the primary years — a proven spiral-learning approach used widely in the United States.
ProgMathematics.t12: From Grade 6 onwards, students advance through pre-algebra, Algebra I, Algebra II, and culminate in Pre-Calculus with an introduction to differentiation and integration by age 16.
ProgMathematics.t13: This trajectory prepares students fully for A-Level or university entrance mathematics.
ProgMathematics.t21: Build a bulletproof mathematical foundation that prepares them for global opportunities, critical thinking, and the highest levels of academia.
ProgMathematics.t44: Pre-Algebra and Algebra I foundations, mastering proportional reasoning and linear equations.
ProgMathematics.t45: Horizons Math Curriculum ensuring solid arithmetic, fractions, decimals and early geometry.
ProgMathematics.t46: Pre-Calculus and an introduction to limits, differentiation, and integration.
ProgMathematics.t47: Algebra I & II alongside formal geometry, quadratics, and trigonometry.

ProgSciences.t7: "Students do not just learn science — they think, investigate, and communicate like scientists."
ProgSciences.t9: Science is taught as an inquiry-driven discipline from the earliest years, transitioning from integrated NGSS-aligned science in Levels 1–5, to disciplinary science from Level 6.
ProgSciences.t10: This culminates in full GCSE/IGCSE preparation with an A-Level bridge in Level 10, giving students a serious academic edge.
ProgSciences.t11: We believe in hands-on exploration, rigorous data analysis, and an appreciation for the precision of the natural world.
ProgSciences.t19: Give your child the scientific literacy to thrive in higher academics and understand the world around them deeply.
ProgSciences.t53: NGSS aligned. Exploring life, earth, and physical sciences through natural curiosity and the scientific method.
ProgSciences.t54: GCSE/IGCSE Foundation and Core years covering extended scientific theory, equations, and required practicals.
ProgSciences.t55: Transition to designated biology, chemistry, and physics tracks with cellular focus and lab techniques.
ProgSciences.t56: GCSE completion, final formal assessments, and specific bridging elements for A-Level sciences.

ProgProgramming.t7: "The emphasis is on problem-solving, project work, and genuine understanding — not rote coding."
ProgProgramming.t9: The Programming curriculum is the most comprehensive in the programme — a ten-level journey that takes students from their very first block of code at age 6 to deploying a real full-stack web application with a professional portfolio by age 16.
ProgProgramming.t10: At every stage, students are engaged in building interactive projects, progressing seamlessly from block-based programming to complex algorithms and data structures using Python and modern web frameworks.
ProgProgramming.t11: We produce engineers who are technically capable, algorithmically literate, and career-ready.
ProgProgramming.t19: From basic logic to deployed web applications, empower your child to create technology, not just consume it.
ProgProgramming.t42: Building responsive frontends and interactivity using modern HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React basics.
ProgProgramming.t43: Scratch Jr and full Scratch 3.0 alongside unplugged computational thinking and game development.
ProgProgramming.t44: Computer Science theory (A-Level prep), Data Science, and a deployed full-stack web application.
ProgProgramming.t45: Transition to text-based Python 3 covering fundamentals, algorithms, data structures, and OOP.
